name: absorption-grade re-measurement of the finite-N chimera collapse-time campaign
description: PR #41's absorption check refuted the original campaign's collapse criterion: theta-crossings sustained for W=5s recover up to 98% of the time (the chimera reforms), so the campaign's 'lifetimes' are first-long-graze times, not absorption times. This module defines an absorption-GRADE criterion (a theta-crossing NOT followed by recovery within a verification horizon T_v), re-measures the full campaign under it from one trace per run (so both the legacy graze label and the new absorption label come from the same integration), and re-runs the survival/aging/geometric analysis to determine which headline results survive. New code only; the shipped voice/supervisor/criterion and all prior results dirs are untouched/read-only.

model._note: beta/dt/sampleStride identical to campaign.config.json so t_graze reproduces the published campaign bit-for-bit (the determinism gate). sampleStride=0.1 is REQUIRED: both the graze criterion (need=round(W/sampleDt) consecutive samples) and the recovery test (round(recWin/sampleDt)) depend on the sample cadence. t_max is the baseline censoring horizon; pilot.mjs measures t_abs censoring at this value and the report records whether it was raised.

graze_criterion._note: UNCHANGED from the published campaign (collapse.mjs DEFAULT_THETA/DEFAULT_W). t_graze = first t0 such that min(R1,R2) > theta sustained for W seconds, else right-censored at t_max. Kept verbatim for the determinism gate and old-vs-new comparability.

absorption_criterion._note: t_abs = the run-start time of the first W-sustained theta-crossing that is NOT followed by recovery within the verification horizon T_v. recovery = min(R1,R2) < recoveryThreshold sustained for >= recoveryWindowSec after the crossing's confirmation (PR #41's CP3 recovery definition). A crossing whose full T_v window is not yet available at t_max leaves the run right-censored for t_abs.

sensitivity._note: CP1 sensitivity knobs swept on the pilot subset; the report states whether tau_hat_abs is robust to these choices or quantifies the movement.

breath._note: PR #41 breath-period estimator (phase.config.json cp2), ported to JS so per-run T_b can be written into every campaign row. T_b = median peak-to-peak interval of R_incoh maxima (smoothed smoothWindowSec, prominence >= minProminenceFrac of the pre-collapse range, min separation minPeakSepFrac * autocorrelation period) over the pre-absorption window EXCLUDING the final (collapsing) cycle. A breath.crosscheck against the Python estimator is reported.

pilot._note: CP2 pilot: estimate t_abs censoring at the baseline t_max and sweep the CP1 sensitivity knobs. Pilot runs integrate to t_max WITHOUT early exit so the largest T_v (240s) verification window is fully available.

phase_subset._note: CP3 phase-clustering + CP4 supervisor-replay subset: the 100 lowest-id seeds per point are re-traced recording R_incoh(t) and R_sync(t)=max(R1,R2) through and past absorption, so true-absorption phase phi_abs and the shipped supervisor's firings can both be computed from the same traces.
